antipattern.symptom: One parametrize block has 47 entries; the test body is 20 lines; reading the test takes longer than running it. Failures are hard to map to intent.
antipattern.root-cause: Author kept adding cases to a single parametrize instead of grouping by category. The test now tests three different concerns at once.
antipattern.fix: Split by category: test_valid_emails, test_invalid_emails, test_disposable_emails — each with ≤ 20 parametrized cases. Spec rule rejects cases.length > 20 per block.

antipattern.symptom: Test collection fails with "DatabaseError: no such table" before any test runs. Pytest cannot even discover the suite.
antipattern.root-cause: Parametrize argument values include Django model instances (`User.objects.create(...)` evaluated at module import). Model instances cannot exist before pytest sets up the test database.
antipattern.fix: Parametrize over IDs, slugs, or dicts; resolve to model instances inside the test using a factory fixture. Spec validator rejects args entries that look like model class references.

antipattern.symptom: pytest failure report: `FAILED tests/test_invoice.py::test_validation[3]`. No clue which case failed without opening the source.
antipattern.root-cause: Author relied on auto-generated numeric IDs from pytest because they didn't use `pytest.param(..., id="name")` or `ids=[]`.
antipattern.fix: Use `pytest.param(value, id="kebab-case-id")` for every row. Spec rule rejects case.id matching `^\d+$`.
